# Figure description: скв_122.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Колонки_ скважин/скв_122.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **well log / borehole geophysical log composite** (in Russian: **Диаграммы каротажа** — "Karotazh diagrams" / wireline logging diagrams). It is a standard Soviet-era геофизический каротаж (geophysical well log) sheet showing multiple geophysical curves recorded along a borehole, accompanied by a lithological column and stratigraphic interpretation. This is one of the most common document types produced during Soviet mineral exploration drilling programs.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a **single borehole (скважина)** logged during the survey. The header references **по бурению** ("by drilling") and **каротаж** ("well logging"). The log appears to cover a substantial depth interval, likely from surface to somewhere in the range of **several hundred meters** (possibly ~1000–1100 m based on the length of the trace and Soviet-era regional survey norms for this area). The borehole is associated with the **northern Kazakhstan** survey area covered by map sheets **M-42 and N-42**.

A reference to **скв** (скважина = borehole) is present in the header, though the specific borehole number is difficult to read clearly at this resolution. The bottom of the log sheet contains a note referencing **контроль каротажа** ("logging quality control") and additional annotation text.

---

## Key Content

The composite log contains the following panels from left to right:

1. **Литологическая колонка (Lithological Column)** — the leftmost panel shows a graphical stratigraphic/lithological column with standard Soviet geological symbols indicating alternating rock types. Symbols suggest interbedded sequences of:
   - Sandstones or siltstones (dotted/stippled patterns)
   - Clays or mudstones (horizontal line patterns)
   - Possibly carbonates or denser rocks (brick-pattern symbols)
   - Some intervals with cross-hatching suggesting crystalline or intrusive rocks at depth

2. **Depth scale** — a vertical depth axis runs along the left margin, graduated in meters. Tick marks and depth values are visible at regular intervals (likely every 10 or 20 m).

3. **Геофизические кривые (Geophysical curves)** — the central portion of the sheet displays multiple overlapping or adjacent geophysical log traces. Based on standard Soviet karotazh practice, these likely include:
   - **КС (кажущееся сопротивление)** — apparent resistivity curves (one or more electrode spacings, e.g., А2М0.5N, А4М1N)
   - **ПС (потенциал самополяризации)** — spontaneous potential (SP) curve
   - **ГК (гамма-каротаж)** — natural gamma ray log
   - Possibly **НГК (нейтронный гамма-каротаж)** — neutron-gamma log
   - Possibly **КВ or КМ** — microresistivity or caliper

4. **The curves show** considerable variation with depth:
   - Numerous sharp deflections in the upper and middle sections suggesting interbedded lithologies (sandstone/clay alternations)
   - A notably more subdued, lower-amplitude section in the middle portion (possibly more homogeneous shale or clay-dominant interval)
   - A zone of high-amplitude anomalies in the lower portion of the logged interval, possibly indicating denser, more resistive rocks (crystalline basement or carbonate horizon)
   - A prominent flat/step in one curve near the bottom suggesting a lithological boundary or tool change

5. **Правый разрез (Right-hand interpreted section)** — to the right of the curves, there is a **geological interpretation column** (проект разреза / stratigraphic section) with formation boundaries marked and possibly formation names annotated.

6. **Far right columns** — contain tabular data, likely including:
   - Formation/layer picks (depths of boundaries)
   - Resistivity values
   - Possibly core recovery percentages or formation names
   - Columns labeled with chemical or physical parameters (possibly Рп, Рн, Кп, or similar petrophysical parameters)

7. **Lower portion** — below the main log, there is a **separate, shorter log panel** (possibly a repeat section or control run — **контроль каротажа**) showing curves over a limited depth interval for quality verification.

## Text and Labels

Visible Russian text, transcribed as accurately as possible at this resolution:

- **Title area (top center):** **ДИАГРАММЫ КАРОТАЖА** ("Karotazh Diagrams" / Well Log Diagrams)
- **Top left:** **ПО БУРЕНИЮ** ("By Drilling" / Drilling Log)
- **Top center-right:** **№ КАРОТАЖА** ("Log Number")
- **Column headers (partial):** likely include **КС**, **ПС**, **ГК**, **НГК**, масштаб глубин (depth scale), масштаб кривых (curve scale)
- **Left column header:** **Литологическая колонка** ("Lithological Column")
- **Right section header:** **ПРОЕКТ РАЗРЕЗА** or **ПРАВЫЙ РАЗРЕЗ** ("Right-hand Section / Project Section")
- **Bottom annotation (near lower log panel):** **Контроль каротажа** ("Logging quality control / repeat section")
- **Bottom right box:** Contains what appears to be a **certification/verification stamp** — standard Soviet геологическая служба documentation block, likely including:
  - Исполнитель (executor/operator)
  - Дата (date)
  - Проверил (checked by)
  - Reference to **Трест** or **Экспедиция** (geological trust or expedition)
- **Depth annotations:** Numerals visible at intervals along the depth track (partially legible: possibly reaching depths of ~600–1100 m)
- **Curve scale headers** at top of log section (partially legible): масштабные значения сопротивления in **Ом·м** (ohm-meters) and ПС in **мВ** (millivolts)
- **Bottom reference:** **Составлена** (compiled) with a date reference
